behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real Git rename detection plus CLI/reporting over an actual repository state (real-infra tag in bounded two-pass). A module-only test can miss Git’s rename heuristics and index/worktree interactions. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML dependency and no agentic multi-step pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real Git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert stdout report shows the file only in `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real Git diff behavior for binary blobs and real CLI/process execution (real-infra tag in bounded two-pass). Mocks often hide parser/encoding/subprocess edge failures. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no model-output dependence and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp real repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI subprocess over the changed range, assert exit code is success and report includes binary-file counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is tied to real repository state (`HEAD` unborn) and CLI handling of Git/subprocess errors (real-infra tag in bounded two-pass). In-process fakes can miss actual no-commit Git semantics. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML call and no agentic chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize an empty Git repo with no commits, invoke the CLI as a subprocess, and assert successful execution with an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on end-to-end interaction with real Git history traversal and actual repository state (real-infra tag in bounded two-pass). Mocked history can drift from real Git behavior and hide failures. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not involve AI/ML output quality or multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo with a scripted sequence of add/delete/rename/modify commits, run the CLI subprocess for the target range, and compare report counts to independently computed ground truth from Git commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This is about real index vs working-tree semantics and CLI behavior against actual Git states (real-infra tag in bounded two-pass). Module wiring tests can miss subtle staged/unstaged differences only visible with real Git plumbing. Rejected higher levels: no model-output dependency and no multi-step agentic orchestration in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change scenarios, run the CLI subprocess for each scenario, and assert normalized structured stdout is identical.
